_doc.hook_md_contract: T1171: Each hook script ('.ps1') has a companion '.md' self-description file at the same path (e.g. '.claude/hooks/g-hk-session-start.md' for '.claude/hooks/g-hk-session-start.ps1'). The hook.md uses the 5-section template (# Hook / ## Fires On / ## What It Does / ## Side Effects / ## Related Tasks) defined by '/g-hook-create'. The runtime contract is: when a hook fires under PreToolUse (or any other event), the harness SHOULD read the matching hook.md and inject its content as additional_context so the agent knows what the hook just did or why a tool call was blocked. See 'skills/g-skl-platform-claude/SKILL.md' Section 3 and 'skills/g-skl-platform-cursor/SKILL.md' Section 11.
_doc.hook_md_authoring: Use '/g-hook-create <hook-name> <event>' to scaffold a new pair atomically. See 'commands/g-hook-create.md'.
_doc.hook_md_source_of_truth: Source the human-facing hook description from the 'hook.md' file; this 'hooks.json' is for wiring only.
_doc.gald3r_lifecycle_events: T1055: In addition to the harness-native events wired above ('sessionStart', 'stop', 'beforeShellExecution', 'PreToolUse'), gald3r defines four gald3r-internal lifecycle events: 'pre_skill', 'post_skill', 'pre_session', 'post_session'. Cursor and Claude Code do NOT expose native skill-boundary or gald3r-session-boundary events, so these are dispatched by the gald3r skill/command runner (or fired manually) and are NOT auto-wired here. Skill events carry 'skill_name', 'skill_path', 'timestamp'; session events carry 'session_id' (if available) and 'project_path'. Reference example hooks: 'g-hk-pre-skill-timing', 'g-hk-post-skill-timing', 'g-hk-pre-session-trace', 'g-hk-post-session-trace' (each with a companion hook.md). See 'commands/g-create-hook.md' for the full event list and 'skills/g-skl-platform-claude/SKILL.md' Section 3.
_doc._gap_claude_hook_config: PLATFORM_SPEC.md (T1462) Section 6 / Section 9: Claude Code's OFFICIAL hook config surface is settings.json under the 'hooks' key, with CAPITALIZED lifecycle event names (SessionStart, Stop, PreToolUse, PostToolUse, UserPromptSubmit, Notification, SubagentStop, PreCompact, SessionEnd) in a matcher-grouped shape. The lowercase 'sessionStart'/'stop'/'beforeShellExecution' entries below are Cursor-era names and MAY SILENTLY NOT FIRE on current Claude Code; 'beforeShellExecution' has NO official Claude Code equivalent. The 'PreToolUse' block below already uses the correct nested {matcher, hooks:[{type:'command',command}]} shape and is the most likely to fire. This file is PRESERVED (not migrated) pending a hook-firing smoke test; do NOT delete working config. Follow-up: consolidate verified-firing entries onto settings.json 'hooks'. See platforms/.claude/README.md and PLATFORM_SPEC.md.

hooks._gap_sessionStart: SUSPECT (PLATFORM_SPEC §6/§9 gap #1): lowercase 'sessionStart' is the Cursor-era name; the official Claude Code event is 'SessionStart' in settings.json. May not fire here. Verify before relying on it.
hooks._gap_stop: SUSPECT (PLATFORM_SPEC §6/§9 gap #1/#2): lowercase 'stop' is the Cursor-era name; the official Claude Code event is 'Stop' in settings.json (where a live chat-logger Stop hook may already exist -> possible double-fire/no-fire ambiguity). Verify before relying on it.
hooks._gap_beforeShellExecution: SUSPECT (PLATFORM_SPEC §6/§9 gap #1): 'beforeShellExecution' is a Cursor-era event name with NO official Claude Code equivalent. The closest Claude Code mechanism is a 'PreToolUse' matcher targeting the Bash tool (see the PreToolUse 'Bash|Shell|...' block below). This entry likely does NOT fire on Claude Code; preserved pending verification.
hooks._gap_PreToolUse: OK (PLATFORM_SPEC §6): this block uses the correct official nested shape {matcher, hooks:[{type:'command',command}]} and is the most likely to fire on Claude Code.
